English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Marion Howe papers
Howe, Marion.
6858
1.7 cubic feet. 1.7 cubic feet.
1879-1990. Correspondence, Newspapers Correspondence, newspapers clippings and memoranda pertaining to Howe's work with the Cornell United Religious Work (CURW) and family. Collection material in English 6631699
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Eileen Keating
Date completed:
October 5, 2009
EAD encoding:
James Zhang, October 5, 2009
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, December 2017
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Cornell class of 1938.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, newspapers clippings and memoranda pertaining to Howe's work with the Cornell United Religious Work (CURW). Also includes family correspondence, largely relating to Delaware County, N.Y., especially letters between her father Harley Howe and her mother; photographs;, papers; and a sewing kit, baby bonnet, mittens and bib.
Cite As:
Marion Howe papers, #6858. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Delaware County (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Howe, Harley Earl, 1882- Howe family. Cornell United Religious Work Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. Cornell University. Class of 1938 Women
